key_insight: Increasing the barrel filling ratio from 43% to 53% made the detonation-sprayed Cr3C2–NiCr coating denser and more wear-resistant, although friction increased.
engineering_relevance: Optimizing detonation-spraying fill ratio is a practical lever for improving protective carbide coatings used in high-wear and high-temperature components.
method: Detonation-sprayed Cr3C2–NiCr coatings at 43% and 53% barrel filling ratios
Characterized microstructure and phase composition using microscopy and XRD
Evaluated dry sliding wear with ball-on-disc tests at 10 and 15 N and 5 and 10 cm/s
evidence_signal: The 53% coating showed the lowest reported wear rate, 1.23 × 10^-4 mm3/(m·N), alongside reduced porosity and better structural integrity.
watch_item: Higher barrel filling ratio improved wear resistance in this study, but the tradeoff was a higher coefficient of friction, so application-specific balance is needed.
paper_title: Effect of Barrel Filling Ratio on the Microstructure, Phase Composition and Tribological Performance of Detonation-Sprayed Cr3C2–NiCr Coatings
